key_insight: PREDIKTOR predicts cancer therapy response by aligning a patient-specific knowledge graph with a learned drug-induced transcriptomic perturbation view.
clinical_relevance: It aims to improve precision oncology by making pre-treatment drug response prediction more accurate, interpretable, and transferable across patients and trials.
method: Builds an individualized gene regulatory network from tumor expression and adds drug-target links from DrugBank.
Uses a graph neural encoder for a mechanistic drug-centered embedding and a pretrained LINCS-based model to simulate post-treatment gene expression.
Aligns both views with a CLIP-style contrastive loss and classifies response from the combined representation.
evidence_signal: The model outperformed strong baselines on TCGA across patient-, drug-, and tissue-split tests and transferred zero-shot to I-SPY2, improving AUROC by 5.6%.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of inferred networks and pretrained perturbation models, so external validation and robustness checks are important before clinical use.
paper_title: Predicting Therapeutic Outcome via Aligning Patient-Specific Knowledge Graph and Gene-Level Perturbation Representations
